sections.heading: Predicate
sections.body:
`cadre_project` with `action: "topology"` reports whether the project uses local
or shared control-plane sync. Shared sync applies equally to monorepo and
polyrepo projects. Product code publication is separate and happens only through
ship or land packets.

sections.heading: Preamble
sections.body:
Before any workflow mutates Cadre state, call the workflow packet requested by
the protocol. The packet is responsible for shared-mode preflight, remote
verification, native state sync, merge-driver health, conflict detection, and
bounded retry policy.

If the packet reports a sync conflict, halt and summarize the conflicting files
or state categories from the packet result. Do not hand-merge Cadre files
state, or generated state JSON.

sections.heading: Postamble
sections.body:
After a workflow packet mutates Cadre state, use the next packet named by the
result for sync or publication. `cadre_project` with
`action: "sync_control_plane"` owns shared control-plane publication and refuses
to publish when product-code changes are mixed into the control-plane surface.

sections.heading: Conflict Policy
sections.body:
- Spec, plan, repository config, provider config, and topology conflicts are
  surfaced to the user by packet results.
- Ephemeral worker state and native local state details are MCP-owned and regenerated or
  repaired only through packets.
- Track-id collisions, shared-mode retries, and sync safety classification are
  handled by packets. Agents report the packet result and stop when it blocks.
